rationale: A Dragon Ball-style Super Saiyan transformation hook is fundamentally misaligned with Sand Loop: the high-energy shounen anime aesthetic (fiery oranges, dark blues, screaming voiceover) clashes with the calm, ASMR, pastel sky-blue/white sorting fantasy, and there's no narrative bridge from "power-up punch" to tap-to-sort-sand color matching. The audience overlap is also weak — anime action fans skew younger/male and seek combat catharsis, not satisfying pixel-art reveals. Skip this archetype; if leadership insists on testing "transformation" as an emotional pitch, reframe it as a sand-pile-to-pixel-art reveal transformation rather than borrowing IP-adjacent action tropes (also IP-risk concern with Goku likeness).
